desc: Copenhagen pairs clean Nordic design with bike-friendly streets, canal districts, and a famously strong food scene. It is ideal for city breaks thanks to compact neighborhoods, waterfront culture, and easy access to museums, parks, and day trips.
hls: Harbor swimming culture in warm months

todo.name: Nyhavn to Kongens Nytorv walk
todo.desc: Start at the iconic canal facades, then continue into central squares and shopping streets.

todo.name: Bike the harbor bridges and neighborhoods
todo.desc: Rent a city bike to connect Vesterbro, Nørrebro, and Christianshavn the local way.

todo.name: Rosenborg Castle and King’s Garden
todo.desc: Tour royal chambers and crown jewels, then relax in one of the city’s favorite green spaces.

todo.name: Torvehallerne food market
todo.desc: Sample smørrebrød, pastries, coffee, and seasonal Danish ingredients under one roof.

todo.name: Designmuseum Danmark or Louisiana Museum
todo.desc: Choose central design collections or a half-day coastal museum trip north of the city.

todo.name: Evening at Tivoli Gardens
todo.desc: Enjoy classic rides, lights, and concerts in the historic amusement park beside the central station.

wifi.note: Very reliable in hotels, cafés, and public spaces; excellent for remote work.

prac.fltNote: Gatwick–Copenhagen has regular nonstop service on multiple carriers throughout the year.
prac.visa: Denmark is in Schengen; many visitors (including US/UK) can stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period visa-free.
alerts: Hotel prices rise sharply during summer festivals and large trade events.

sweet: June to August has long days and waterfront life at its best, while shoulder months are cooler but calmer.
